name: Macbeth
pattern: Loyal warrior visited by witches with a prophecy; pushed by his wife to seize the kingdom by murdering the trusting king under his own roof; one murder forces the next, then the next; sleep murdered, friends suspicious; fall on the field still trying to outwit a prophecy.
failure_mode: Treating a prophecy of future possibility as a license for present violence.
restoration_path: None taken; refused the door of guilt back to honest confession.
markers: fights to the end inside his own delusion

name: Hamlet
pattern: Prince called by his fathers ghost to right a regicide; tests the kings conscience with a play; thinks too long, kills the wrong man behind the curtain; the ripple of his delay drowns everyone he loves.
failure_mode: Endless verification when action is required; rumination weaponized.
restoration_path: The duty was given; the delay was the failure mode.
markers: kills the wrong man through delay
accepts his own death at the end

name: King Lear
pattern: Aged king demands his daughters perform their love before he divides the kingdom; banishes the one who refuses theater (Cordelia); discovers too late that the flattering daughters intended his fall; goes mad on the heath; reconciles with Cordelia at the very end of his strength.
failure_mode: Confusing the performance of love with love.
restoration_path: On the heath the king becomes naked man and learns what he could not learn on the throne.
markers: gives away the strength that protected him

name: Captain Ahab
pattern: One-legged captain pursues a single white whale across the worlds oceans; conscripts his entire crew into his monomania; refuses the help offered, refuses the sane voyage; goes down lashed to the thing he hunted.
failure_mode: Personal vendetta dressed as commission; the entire crew sacrificed to one mans wound.
restoration_path: Could have turned back at any port.
markers: ignores warnings from the lawful trade

name: Don Quixote
pattern: Aging gentleman reads too many chivalry romances; sallies forth to right wrongs and defend ladies in a world that no longer obeys those rules; mistakes windmills for giants; is mocked, beaten, and finally restored to ordinary sanity on his deathbed when he renounces the books.
failure_mode: Living inside a literary order that no longer matches the world; refusing the reality test.
restoration_path: Renunciation on the deathbed; sanity is itself a confession.
markers: consumes one genre until it possesses him
mistakes ordinary objects for the genres figures
sane on the deathbed and renounces the cause

name: Raskolnikov
pattern: Brilliant impoverished student theorizes a class of extraordinary men exempt from moral law; murders the pawnbroker to prove the theory; is undone not by detection but by his own conscience and by Sonya; eventually confesses at the crossroads, accepts Siberia, and begins to live.
failure_mode: A philosophy that exempts the self from the moral order.
restoration_path: Kneel at the crossroads; kiss the earth; confess publicly; accept the sentence; begin reading the Gospel.
markers: commits the act to prove the theory

name: Atticus Finch
pattern: Small-town Southern lawyer assigned to defend Tom Robinson in an unwinnable case; takes it seriously when most expected a token defense; is despised by neighbors, threatened, his children put at risk; loses the verdict; is mourned by the black community as he leaves the courtroom.
restoration_path: Defend the case as if the verdict could be just; raise the children to see it.
markers: assigned a case the town wants lost
mounts a real defense in losing

name: Jean Valjean
pattern: Convict released after nineteen years for stealing bread; steals silver from a bishop who has shown him hospitality; caught, the bishop tells the police he gave the silver freely and adds the candlesticks; the mercy reorders the man for life; relentlessly hunted by Javert, he saves enemies and adopts an orphan; dies forgiven.
failure_mode: Was caught in the act and did not deserve the mercy.
restoration_path: Receive a mercy that does not fit you; let it reorder the rest of your life.
markers: mercy worth more than the theft

name: Javert
pattern: Inspector who has built his whole identity on the law as an absolute; cannot integrate the mercy Valjean shows him by sparing his life; the order of his world collapses; throws himself into the Seine.
failure_mode: Law without grace becomes its own idol; cannot survive grace.
restoration_path: Could have received the mercy as a revision of the order rather than its end.
markers: spared by the one he pursued
ends his own life rather than be remade

name: Frodo Baggins
attribution: Tolkien, The Lord of the Rings
pattern: Quiet hobbit handed an inheritance he did not want and cannot refuse; carries it toward Mount Doom across a wounded world; at the very edge cannot complete the act; the providence of Gollum and the long mercy of Bilbo bring the ring to the fire anyway; never fully heals from the wound; sails to the West.
failure_mode: At the cracks, his own strength fails — but the failure itself is folded into the providence by long-prior mercy.
restoration_path: Carry it as far as you can; trust that the mercies extended earlier still work at the end.
markers: mercy from years before finishes the work

name: Boromir
attribution: Tolkien, The Lord of the Rings
pattern: Captain of Gondor; argues throughout for using the ring; finally tries to take it from Frodo; the deed broken, returns to himself and dies defending the two hobbits he had earlier despised; confesses to Aragorn at the end.
failure_mode: Convinced himself the strong should wield what was forbidden.
restoration_path: After the failure, defend the small ones to the death and confess clearly.

name: Sydney Carton
attribution: Dickens, A Tale of Two Cities
pattern: Dissolute lawyer in love with a woman he believes himself unworthy of; trades places at the guillotine for her husband, his look-alike; goes to the blade saying it is a far, far better thing.
failure_mode: A wasted life before the trade.
restoration_path: Spend the wasted life in one decisive act for the one you cannot have.
markers: trades self for the loved ones beloved

name: Miss Havisham
pattern: Jilted at the altar; stops every clock in the house at twenty minutes to nine; sits in her bridal gown for decades growing the cake into a ruin; raises Estella as an instrument of revenge on men; burns at the end; dies asking forgiveness.
failure_mode: Building a life around the moment of betrayal; weaponizing the next generation.
restoration_path: Burn the room down and ask forgiveness — but very late.
markers: clocks stopped at the wounds hour
raises another to repeat the harm

name: Ivan Ilyich
attribution: Tolstoy, The Death of Ivan Ilyich
pattern: Career bureaucrat who lived correctly and pleasantly; falls ill from a trifling injury; the doctors and family lie politely as he dies; only the peasant Gerasim is honest with him; at the very end realizes his correct life was wrong and dies in light.
failure_mode: A pleasant correct life that was not the right life.
restoration_path: Be honest in the dying; let the lowly servant tell the truth the family will not.

name: Prince Myshkin
pattern: Returns from a Swiss sanitarium with the simplicity of a child; sees through the calculations of Petersburg society without trying; is loved by two women and cannot wound either; is broken by the world he cannot lie to; ends in renewed insanity.
failure_mode: None on his side; the world cannot tolerate the unguarded sight.
restoration_path: Speak the truth in love and be broken by it if you must.
markers: broken by the world he refused to lie to
